notes:
Tried multiple fresh proton versions, including GE-Proton8-3, GE-Proton7-43 (the one the devs say is validated), Proton Experimental, Proton-8.0-2, Proton 7.0-6, and Proton-6.3-8
Also tried installing VC 2022 (on many of the aforementioned proton versions) through winetricks AND running the windows provided installer through `protontricks -c 'wine ...'`

Their discord has tons of people facing similar anti-cheat related issues, the same and different. It does not appear to be proton-specific at this point in time. However, they are also planning on moving to FACEIT anti-cheat (for their primary servers) which will force linux players to only play on community servers. Of which not all will community servers will work as they can rent servers from a linked provider to utilize FACEIT anti-cheat and make it even more difficult for linux players to decipher which servers are usable

notes:
It was working most of the year. I am as of the 2.2.4 update getting the error
"Failed to initialize graphics.
Make sure you have DirectX 11 installed, have up to date
drivers for your graphics card and have not disabled
3D acceleration in display settings.
InitializeEngineGraphics failed"

This has been remedied in the past by finding a version of proton compatible that may be one of a number of versions. Other games I have played will have that error and are remedied by having a specific version. As of now I do not know a version that will allow me to move past the error.

It will show the loading screen for initializing the screen before crashing. Hopefully there is a proton version that will work again in the future with this game as it is one I really enjoy playing with friends.
